title: I built an AI-driven live terminal that detects geopolitical & financial blindspots before they hit the news. Here is how it works.
author: Alternative_Job9720
selftext:
Hey everyone,

I’ve spent the last few months coding **All-Trace**, a real-time intelligence terminal designed to capture weak signals before they become mainstream breaking news.

As a solo dev, I was tired of "AI tools" that just summarize old articles. I wanted something proactive. So I built an engine that continuously crunches multi-layered data—cross-media semantics, state-sponsored propaganda, and on-chain blockchain movements (specifically heavy stablecoin flows in conflict zones)—to predict asymmetric market risks.

# ⚡ How it works under the hood:

Instead of static dashboards, All-Trace is built as a **Single Live Feed Augmented by Agents**.

1. **Data Ingestion:** The system tracks closed sources, maritime transponders, local regional state media in native languages, and crypto smart contracts.
2. **The Semantic Friction Engine:** An AI agent instantly compares what a state-owned media outlet says in its native language versus its international English channel. If there's a major statistical divergence in tone or facts, the terminal triggers a *"Friction Alert"*.
3. **The Mirror Effect:** When an event occurs, the model doesn't just look at the epicenter. It simulates the domino effect—calculating how a tech factory incident in Asia impacts logistic routes in Europe or specific tech stocks within 6 hours.

# 📊 The "Proof of Work" (Why I'm posting this)

I don’t want you to take my word for it. The terminal features a public **Track Record Ledger**. Every single predictive risk score generated by the AI is logged and time-stamped. When the crisis actually hits the mainstream press, the system calculates the exact "Time Advantage" the AI had over standard journalism. (Current average is about 8 to 14 hours ahead on specific regional supply chain disruptions).

comments.author: pr0XYTV
comments.body: Having AI write your descriptions on you AI coded AI driven program is wild work

comments.author: Dry_Pomegranate4911
comments.body: So, do you have anything to share that we could look at? I’ve been wondering to build something similar
